body.title: CLOSED: Refactor pe_chain phases: shared basket dict → explicit typed phase contracts
body.result: TypedDicts added for all 12 pe_chain phases; module-level shims annotated; 5 independence tests for pe_hypothesize passing; 309 existing tests unchanged
body.description:
pe_chain phases all read/write a shared mutable basket dict, making every phase implicitly coupled to every prior phase — any key set upstream can be read downstream, so phases can't be swapped, skipped, or tested independently. This is the primary reason the cognition pipeline is hard to modify without unexpected cross-phase side effects.

Refactor so each phase declares explicit typed inputs and outputs (Python TypedDict is the natural fit — one TypedDict per phase interface). The chain assembles the next phase's input from the prior phase's output rather than passing the entire mutable dict through. Existing behavior must be preserved — this is a structural refactor, not a feature change. Phase interfaces should be minimal: only the keys each phase actually reads/writes, not the full basket.

**Affected files:** ~/TheIgors/wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (primary); tests/test_pe_chain.py or equivalent (test update)
**Design rules:** OOP-first when shared state crosses functions (palace: theigors/rules/coding); MEDIUM inertia (cognition pipeline — not brainstem); no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** pe_chain phase-to-phase contracts only. Entry point (basket initialization from ticket) may need updating to return the first phase's typed input. Out of scope: NE internals, other pipeline components, changing phase logic. Behavior-preserving refactor only — no new capabilities.
**Test plan:** All existing pe_chain tests pass unchanged after refactor; at least one phase (e.g. pe_hypothesize) has a unit test that constructs its typed input directly and asserts on its typed output without running the full pipeline — demonstrating the independence property.
